tasks.task: I’ve got a worksheet with a few directions that aren’t super clear at first glance, and I want to make sure I do it right. Can you help me figure out a good way to break down the instructions and check I’m not missing anything?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school/worksheet scene and invites the user to describe how they handle written directions, which should naturally reveal whether they understand them reliably.
tasks.target_short: Understands written instructions reliably

tasks.task: I need to send a short message to a teacher about why I’m turning something in late, but I want it to sound clear and not overdone. Can you help me draft it so it gets the point across without making it awkward?
tasks.rationale: This asks for a practical written message in a school setting, which should prompt the user to show how clearly she can explain herself and what level of writing polish she naturally uses.
tasks.target_short: Writes clearly enough to explain herself

tasks.task: I’ve got a math quiz coming up and I want to study without wasting time on the wrong stuff. Can you help me make a quick plan for what to practice and how to check if I actually understand it?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school-studying scene and invites the user to describe what kinds of math practice they can handle, which should naturally surface their real level of competence and confidence with routine school math.
tasks.target_short: Handles school math with steady competence

tasks.task: I’ve got a busy week coming up with school stuff and a couple of other obligations, and I don’t want to get behind. Can you help me map out a simple plan for the next few days so I can keep up without feeling rushed?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school-and-obligations planning scene that naturally invites the user to describe how they budget their time, revealing whether they plan ahead, adjust routines, or juggle homework and other commitments.
tasks.target_short: Plans around schoolwork and obligations

tasks.task: I’m trying to decide between saving up for something I really want and spending a little on fun stuff this month. Can you help me think through a simple way to split my money so I don’t regret it later?
tasks.rationale: This uses a budgeting/planning scene and invites the user to naturally mention whether they save steadily, avoid impulse spending, or prioritize a bigger goal over immediate purchases.
tasks.target_short: Saves carefully for goals

tasks.task: I’m trying to get my school stuff together for a day when the weather might turn bad, and I don’t want my charger or papers getting ruined. Can you help me think through a simple way to pack it so everything stays usable and I’m not digging around later?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school-and-weather packing scene that naturally invites the user to mention how they protect materials and organize routine items, which should surface their practical material-handling habits.
tasks.target_short: Keeps school and work items organized

tasks.task: I’m trying to sort out a school project and a couple of homework tasks for this week, but I know plans can change at the last minute. Can you help me think through a simple way to keep moving if one part of the day gets interrupted?
tasks.rationale: This sets up a normal planning problem with a realistic interruption, so the user’s reply can naturally show whether they handle setbacks smoothly without directly naming that capability.
tasks.target_short: Stays functional when plans go wrong

tasks.task: I’m helping a friend understand what to do in a small retail job, and I’m not sure what the normal next step is. If a cashier notices a pay mistake or someone turns in a lost item, what’s the usual process for getting it to the right person?
tasks.rationale: This uses a retail/workplace scene and invites the user to explain ordinary store procedures and who handles corrections or found items, which should surface their practical knowledge of basic workplace operations.
tasks.target_short: Knows basic workplace and store operations

tasks.task: I’m stuck on a homework problem and want to make sure I’m not missing something basic. It says a rectangle has a perimeter of 30 and a length of 9 — can you help me figure out the width and explain the steps so I can check my work?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school-math homework scene and invites the user to respond with their own understanding or correction of the setup and steps, which should naturally surface solid basic math knowledge.
tasks.target_short: Has solid basic school math knowledge

tasks.task: I need to figure out the safest way to get to school on a day when my usual ride might not work out. Can you help me think through a backup plan and what details I should check first?
tasks.rationale: This should prompt the user to naturally mention how they handle local transit changes, including bus delays or cancellations, while using a school-commute planning scene.
tasks.target_short: Understands local commuting disruptions

tasks.task: I need to figure out the easiest way to get from my school area to a couple of nearby places without overcomplicating it. Can you help me map out a simple route plan and point out what I should check first so I don’t end up taking the long way?
tasks.rationale: This uses a local-navigation scene and should prompt the user to mention nearby streets, landmarks, or walking routes they know around Encanto, revealing neighborhood-level geographic familiarity in their reply.
tasks.target_short: Knows her neighborhood-level area well

tasks.task: I’ve got a school day coming up where the forecast looks a little messy, and I’m trying to decide what I should plan for ahead of time. Can you help me think through what to check first so I’m not scrambling later?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school/travel planning scene and invites the user to naturally mention rain-related concerns and precautions, which should surface their weather-and-seasonal knowledge.
tasks.target_short: Watches rain and flood risk closely

tasks.task: I’m trying to figure out whether a part-time shift I worked was paid correctly, but I’m not sure how to check it without making it a big deal. Can you help me think through the basic math I should use and what numbers I’d compare first?
tasks.rationale: This uses a part-time job/paycheck scene and should prompt the user to naturally mention wage and hours basics, revealing whether they understand simple paycheck checking and underpayment.
tasks.target_short: Understands saving and paycheck basics

tasks.task: I think I got paid wrong for a shift, and I’m not sure what I should have ready before I ask someone to fix it. Can you help me figure out the best way to handle it without making it a big thing?
tasks.rationale: This uses a work/pay scene and invites the user to mention checking pay details and bringing proof, which should surface their everyday legal-awareness knowledge about wage issues.
tasks.target_short: Knows to verify pay and return lost property

tasks.task: I’m behind on a worksheet because my evening got disrupted, and I need to let my teacher know I won’t have it ready on time. What’s a simple way to explain that and ask what I should do next?
tasks.rationale: A routine school delay creates a natural opening for the user to mention a real past time when being straightforward about a disruption led to practical help from a teacher.
tasks.target_short: Teacher helped her after she told the truth

tasks.task: We’ve got a few days of messy weather coming up, and I need to think through what to do if my usual ride to school isn’t available. Can you help me map out a simple backup plan and what I should check ahead of time?
tasks.rationale: A weather-related school-transport planning prompt naturally invites the user to mention a past time when bad weather disrupted getting to school and what they learned from it.
tasks.target_short: Walked to school through flooded roads

tasks.task: I just got my first paycheck and I’m trying to make sure everything adds up before I spend anything. Can you tell me the easiest way to check it against my hours so I know whether it looks right?
tasks.rationale: This uses a work/paycheck scene that naturally invites the user to mention a past time they noticed a pay mistake and became more careful about checking pay.
tasks.target_short: Found she had been underpaid at work

tasks.task: I think my hours on a small work shift might be off, and I want to handle it the right way without making it awkward. Can you help me figure out what to bring up first and how to explain it clearly?
tasks.rationale: This work/payroll situation is likely to prompt the user to mention a similar past experience of fixing a pay mistake with a manager, revealing the target episodic memory through a natural reply.
tasks.target_short: Corrected a payroll mistake with her manager

tasks.task: I found a wallet near the front desk at school, and I’m trying to figure out the right way to turn it in. What should I check first so it gets back to the right person?
tasks.rationale: A found-wallet situation naturally invites the user to describe what they did in a real past incident, including how they identified the owner and handed it to the proper person.
tasks.target_short: Returned a found wallet to the manager

tasks.task: I’ve got a school week where I’m trying to keep up with homework while also dealing with a couple of late afternoons, and I don’t want my grades to slip. Can you help me make a simple plan for staying on top of everything without burning out?
tasks.rationale: This sets up a school-and-workload pressure situation that invites the user to naturally mention a past time they balanced after-school work with steady grades; the scene is school/work scheduling.
tasks.target_short: Kept grades steady while working after school

tasks.task: I’ve got a few school things piling up this week, and the weather might make the schedule less predictable. Can you help me think through what I should get ready ahead of time so I’m not scrambling if plans change?
tasks.rationale: A changing schedule and weather-related disruption naturally prompt the user to mention any past experience that led them to prepare more carefully, without explicitly naming that conclusion in the prompt.
tasks.target_short: Realized she needed a stricter routine

tasks.task: I need to send a quick message to a teacher about a mistake on an assignment, and I want it to be clear enough that they know what happened. Can you help me phrase it in a way that gets the point across without leaving out anything important?
tasks.rationale: This sets up a common school communication problem where a direct, factual reply naturally reveals whether the user tends to describe mistakes plainly and plainly own them.
tasks.target_short: Sees herself as straightforward and truthful

tasks.task: I’m working on a school assignment and I have enough time to look it over once more before I hand it in. I’m trying to decide what to check first so I can finish it without missing anything important.
tasks.rationale: This keeps the schoolwork-and-deadline situation, so a reply can naturally reveal whether the user prefers to review details and plan ahead without directly naming that trait.
tasks.target_short: Sees herself as careful and measured

tasks.task: I’m trying to make a realistic study plan for this week because I’ve got a few assignments and I don’t want to fall behind if one day gets weird. Can you help me set up something simple that I can stick with even if I’m tired?
tasks.rationale: This invites the user to describe how they handle workload and setbacks, which can naturally surface that she sees herself as someone who keeps working; the scene is school/study planning.
tasks.target_short: Sees herself as someone who keeps working

tasks.task: I’ve got a school project coming up, and there’s a point where I’m not sure whether to keep going on my own or check in with someone for a quick answer. Can you help me think through the situation and what details I should look at first?
tasks.rationale: This sets up a normal school decision point that naturally invites the user to reveal whether they tend to rely on their own effort or seek input, without naming or presupposing that stance.
tasks.target_short: Anchored by independence and effort

tasks.task: I’m trying to decide whether to tell my teacher that I can take on the group part of a project even if the others are being flaky. Can you help me think through whether that’s a smart move and what I should say if I volunteer?
tasks.rationale: This uses a school group-project scene and invites the user to frame herself as someone who can step up and follow through, which can naturally surface whether she sees herself as reliable when it counts.
tasks.target_short: Sees herself as reliable when it counts

tasks.task: I need to message a teacher about a grade that looks off on one of my assignments, and I want to make sure I explain it clearly. Can you help me draft a short note that sounds respectful and gets the point across?
tasks.rationale: A grade-check message naturally invites the user to choose the tone and level of polish they want, which will reveal their preferred style of help without naming it in the prompt.
tasks.target_short: Prefers a neutral, not-too-formal tone

tasks.task: I need to message a teacher about one small issue on an assignment, and I want to make sure I include the right detail without overexplaining. Can you help me draft a note that gets the point across clearly?
tasks.rationale: A brief school-message situation naturally invites the user to show how much detail they want in the help, without directly naming or presupposing their preference for brevity.
tasks.target_short: Wants concise help with only what matters

tasks.task: I’ve got a couple of school things I need to sort out and I’m feeling a little scattered. Can you help me figure out where to start so I can get moving without missing anything important?
tasks.rationale: This keeps the same school-planning situation and the need for guidance under mild stress, which naturally invites the user to respond with the kind of help format they find easiest to use.
tasks.target_short: Likes help in manageable steps

tasks.task: I’m trying to decide whether to join a school club, and I need to think it through before the deadline. Can you help me sort out the main things I should check so I can make a decision?
tasks.rationale: This keeps the school-club decision situation, which naturally invites the user to reveal how much detail and structure they want in help, without explicitly asking for a brief or direct response.
tasks.target_short: Prefers straightforward answers without fuss

tasks.task: I need help writing a short message to a teacher about an assignment issue. Can you give me a draft that gets the point across clearly and stays easy to send?
tasks.rationale: A simple school message request naturally invites the user to choose the tone and level of detail they prefer, which will reveal whether they want calm, matter-of-fact wording.
tasks.target_short: Likes calm, matter-of-fact support
